chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي أَنَّ الصَّدَقَةَ تَزِيدُ فِي الْمَالِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 52 – Regarding that the charity increased in the wealth

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/135)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/135)
text: مَا أَحْسَنَ عَبْدٌ الصَّدَقَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا أَحْسَنَ اللَّهُ الْخِلَافَةَ عَلَى وُلْدِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ قَالَ حُسْنُ الصَّدَقَةِ يَقْضِي الدَّيْنَ وَ يَخْلُفُ عَلَى الْبَرَكَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A servant does not give good charity in the world except that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> betters the succession upon his children from after him’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Good charity pays off the debts, and leaves behind the Blessings’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father, from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Whenever a man gives charity of the best quality, Allah replaces it for him with children in the same way (of best quality children) after his passing away.” The Imam has also said, ‘Giving charity of best quality pays off debts and leaves behind blessings.’”
